blocks:
سُورة الفرقان

مكّيّة وهي 77 آية

(١) **تَبارَكَ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ الْفُرْقانَ عَلى عَبْدِهِ ** (1) Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His servant,

His goodness multiplied, derived from البركة (blessing), which is the abundance of goodness. The interpretation of الفرقان (the Criterion) has already preceded in Surah Aal 'Imran. لِيَكُونَ ** (So that he may be) the servant (العبد) or the Criterion (الفرقان) لِلْعالَمِينَ نَذِيراً ** (a warner to all the nations) for the jinn and mankind, as a warner (مُنذِراً) or as a warning (إنذاراً), like النكير (denial) meaning الإنكار (denial/reproach).

(2) الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَداً (2) the One to whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and He has not taken any son, as the Christians claimed, وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي الْمُلْكِ (and He has no partner in the sovereignty) as the Dualists (الثنوية) say, وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ فَقَدَّرَهُ تَقْدِيراً and He created everything and determined it with a [precise] determination.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) that he said: Do you know what al-Taqdir (التقدير) is? It was said: No. He said: It is the setting of limits (الحدود) for lifespans (الآجال), provisions (الأرزاق), permanence (البقاء), and annihilation (الفناء). Do you know what al-Qada' (القضاء) is? It was said: No. He said: It is the establishment of the essence (إقامة العين).

(٣) وَاتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ آلِهَةً لا يَخْلُقُونَ شَيْئاً وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ (3) Yet they have taken gods besides Him who create nothing, but are themselves created, because their worshippers carve them (يَنْحَتُونَهُمْ) and fashion their images (وَيُصَوِّرُونَهُمْ). وَلا يَمْلِكُونَ and they are not able to [control] for themselves any harm (ضَرًّا), [meaning] repelling harm (دَفْعَ ضَرٍّ). وَلا نَفْعاً nor attracting benefit (جَلْبَ نَفْعٍ). وَلا يَمْلِكُونَ مَوْتاً وَلا حَياةً وَلا نُشُوراً and they do not control the causing of death (إِمَاتَةَ) to anyone, nor giving him life (وَإِحْيَائِهِ) initially, nor resurrecting him (وَبَعْثِهِ) a second time. And whoever is like that is far removed (فَبِمَعْزِلٍ) from divinity (الْأُلُوهِيَّة).

(٤) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَذا إِلَّا إِفْكٌ افْتَراهُ وَأَعانَهُ عَلَيْهِ قَوْمٌ آخَرُونَ فَقَدْ جاؤُ ظُلْماً وَزُوراً (4) (The faithless say, 'This is nothing but a fabrication that he has forged, and other people have helped him with it. So they have certainly come up with injustice and falsehood.')

وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَذا ** They mean the Quran إِلَّا إِفْكٌ ** a lie (كذب) turned away from its proper aspect (مصروف عن وجهه) افْتَراهُ ** He invented/fabricated it (اختلقه) وَأَعانَهُ عَلَيْهِ قَوْمٌ آخَرُونَ.

Al-Qummi said: They said, "This which the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم reads and informs us about, he only learns it from the Jews and writes it down from the scholars of the Christians, and he writes from a man called Ibn Qubaysah (ابن قبيصة), who transmits it to him in the mornings and evenings." So, He, the Glorified and Exalted, related their saying and refuted them.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): al-Ifk (الافك) is the lie (الكذب). And قَوْمٌ آخَرُونَ ** They mean Abu Fukayhah (أبا فكيهة), a scholar (حبراً), ‘Addas (عداساً), and ‘Abis (عابساً), the freedman (مولى) of Huwaytib (حويطب). فَقَدْ جاؤُ ظُلْماً وَزُوراً**

(٥) وَقالُوا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (5) And they say, 'Myths of the ancients.' What the predecessors inscribed. اكْتَتَبَها ** that he has written down, He wrote it himself or had it written. فَهِيَ تُمْلى عَلَيْهِ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً. and they are dictated to him morning and evening.' Al-Qummi (said this is) the saying of Al-Nadr ibn al-Harith ibn 'Alqamah ibn Kaladah.

(٦) قُلْ أَنْزَلَهُ الَّذِي يَعْلَمُ السِّرَّ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (6) Say, 'He has sent it down who knows the secret in the heavens and the earth.' Because it incapacitated you, even the last of you, with its eloquence, and it contained news about future unseen matters (مغيبات) and hidden things (أشياء مكنونة) which none knows except the Knower of Secrets (عالم الاسرار). So how can you make it tales of the ancients (أساطير الأولين)? إِنَّهُ كانَ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً ** (He is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.) Therefore, He does not hasten your punishment for what you say, despite the perfection of His power and your deserving that He pour down the punishment upon you profusely (صبّاً).

(7) وَقالُوا ما لِهذَا الرَّسُولِ ** (And they said: 'What is the matter with this Apostle') who claims prophethood, and in this [saying] there is contempt and mockery, يَأْكُلُ الطَّعامَ كما نأكل وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْواقِ ** (who eats food as we eat, and walks in the marketplaces) seeking livelihood as we seek it. The meaning is: If his claim is true, then why is his condition not different from our condition? That was due to their blindness (عَمَه) and the limitation of their sight to perceptible things (المحسوسات). For the distinction of the Apostles from others is not by physical matters (أمور جسمانية), but rather by spiritual states (أحوال نفسانية), as He pointed out in His saying: قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ يُوحى إِلَيَّ أَنَّما إِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ (Say: 'I am only a human being like you, who is revealed to that your God is the One God.') **لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُ نَذِيراً ** (Why has not an angel been sent down to him, to be a warner along with him?) so that his truthfulness may be known through the angel's confirmation.

(8) أَوْ يُلْقى إِلَيْهِ كَنْزٌ ** (8) or a treasure be cast down to him so that he may seek strength through it and dispense with the need to earn a livelihood (تحصيل المعاش). أَوْ تَكُونُ لَهُ جَنَّةٌ يَأْكُلُ مِنْها ** or he has a garden from which he may eat as a provision (على سبيل النزل). That is, if a treasure is not cast down to him, then the least (He could do) is that he has a garden, like those belonging to the wealthy landowners (الدّهاقين) and the affluent (المياسير), so that he may live off its yield (بريعه). And it was read (وقرء) نأكل (we eat) with the nūn (بالنون). وَقالَ الظَّالِمُونَ إِنْ تَتَّبِعُونَ ** (9) And the wrongdoers say, 'You follow (They say) what you follow is إِلَّا رَجُلاً مَسْحُوراً ** (9) only a man bewitched.' meaning he has been bewitched (سحر) and his intellect has been overcome (فغلب على عقله). It was said (قيل) that الظَّالِمُونَ ** (the wrongdoers) was placed in the position of their pronoun (موضع ضميرهم) in order to register (تسجيلاً) against them the charge of wrongdoing (بالظلم) regarding what they said.

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with this verse thus: وَقالَ الظَّالِمُونَ (And the wrongdoers say) regarding the right (حقّهم) of the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them): إِنْ تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلاً مَسْحُوراً (You follow only a man bewitched).

(٩) انْظُرْ كَيْفَ ضَرَبُوا لَكَ الْأَمْثالَ ** (9) Look how they strike parables for you They spoke concerning you the aberrant sayings (الأقوال الشاذة) and invented for you the rare states (الأحوال النادرة) فَضَلُّوا ** and have gone astray from the path leading to the knowledge of the characteristics (خواص) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and distinguishing between him and the false claimant to prophethood (المتنبي), so they stumbled blindly (فخبطوا خبط عشواء) فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلاً ** so they cannot find a way to disparage your prophethood (نبوتك) or to reach guidance (الرّشد) and direction (الهدى). And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: [The way (السبيل) is] to the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali عليه السلام, and Ali is the Way (السبيل).

(10) Blessed is He Who, if He wishes, will grant you in this world better than that which they mentioned, but He deferred it until the Hereafter because it is better and more enduring: gardens with streams running in their beds, and He will grant you palaces. تَبارَكَ الَّذِي إِنْ شاءَ جَعَلَ لَكَ خَيْراً مِنْ ذلِكَ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكَ قُصُوراً ** (10) Blessed is He Who, if He wishes, will grant you better than that: gardens with streams running in their beds, and He will grant you palaces. And the word يَجْعَلْ (grant) was read with the raf' (nominative case, يَجْعَلُ).

In al-Ihtijaj and Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Imam (peace be upon him) concerning Surat al-Baqarah regarding His saying, the Glorified: أَمْ تُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَسْئَلُوا رَسُولَكُمْ كَما سُئِلَ مُوسى مِنْ قَبْلُ (Do you want to question your Apostle, just as Moses was questioned before?). The Imam (peace be upon him) said: I said to my father, Ali ibn Muhammad (peace be upon them both): Did the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله engage in debate with the Jews and the polytheists when they reproached Him and argue against them? He said: Many times. That was because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was sitting one day in Mecca in the courtyard (فناء) of the Ka'bah, when Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah al-Makhzumi began, saying: "O Muhammad, you have made a great claim and spoken a terrifying statement. You allege that you are the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds, yet it is not fitting for the Lord of the Worlds and the Creator of all creation that His Messenger should be like you—a human being like us, who eats as we eat and walks in the markets وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْواقِ ** as we walk. This King of Rome and this King of Persia do not send a messenger unless he is wealthy, great, and significant, possessing palaces, houses (دُور), tents (فساطيط), pavilions (خيام), slaves (عبيد), and servants (خدّام). And the Lord of the Worlds is above all of them, for they are His servants. If you were a prophet, there would be an angel with you to confirm you, whom we could witness. Rather, if Allah wished to send a prophet to us, He would only send an angel to us, not a human being like us. O Muhammad, you are nothing but a man bewitched (مسحور), and you are not a prophet." Then they proposed many things, the mention of which has passed in Surat Bani Isra'il, and the mention of some of them will come in Surat al-Zukhruf, if Allah wills.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O Allah, You are the Hearer of every sound and the Knower of everything. You know what Your servants have said." So Allah revealed to them, O Muhammad: وَقالُوا ما لِهذَا الرَّسُولِ يَأْكُلُ الطَّعامَ (And they say, 'What sort of apostle is this, who eats food) up to His saying: palaces **قُصُوراً ** along with other verses that have passed.

He said: Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O Abdullah, as for what you mentioned—that I eat food as you eat—and your claim that because of this it is not permissible for me to be a Messenger of Allah, the command belongs only to Allah. He does what He wills and decrees what He desires, and He is Praised. Neither I nor anyone else has the right to object. How can this be, when you do not see how Allah has impoverished some and enriched others, honored some and humiliated others, made some healthy and made others sick, and elevated some and degraded others? All of them are among those who eat food. Then, it is not for the poor to say, 'Why have You impoverished us and enriched them?' Nor for the degraded to say, 'Why have You degraded us and elevated them?' Nor for the chronically ill (الزّمناء) and the weak to say, 'Why have You afflicted us with chronic illness (أزمنتنا) and weakness, and made them healthy?' Nor for the humiliated to say, 'Why have You humiliated us and honored them?' Nor for those with ugly appearances (قباح الصّور) to say, 'Why have You made us ugly and made them beautiful?' Rather, if they were to say that, they would be rejecting their Lord, disputing His decrees, and disbelieving in Him. But His answer to them is: 'I am the King, the Abaser (الخافض), the Exalter (الرافع), the Enricher (المغني), the Impoverisher (المفقر), the Honorer (المعزّ), the Humiliator (المذلّ), the Giver of Health (المصحّح), the Giver of Sickness (المسقم). And you are the servants; you have nothing but submission to Me and compliance with My decree. If you submit, you are believing servants, and if you refuse, you are disbelievers in Me and among the doomed due to My punishments.' Then Allah revealed to Him, O Muhammad: Say: 'I am only a human being like you قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ —meaning, I eat food—it is revealed to me that your God is the One God يُوحى إِلَيَّ أَنَّما إِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ —meaning, say to them: 'I am like you in humanity, but my Lord has singled me out for prophethood, just as He singles out some humans for wealth, health, and beauty, excluding others among humanity. So do not deny that He should also single me out for prophethood.'" Then He answered their other proposals with what was previously mentioned in Surat Bani Isra'il and al-An'am.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "And as for your saying, 'You are nothing but a man bewitched (مسحور),' how can I be so, when you know that I surpass you in the soundness of discernment (التّميز) and intellect? Have you ever experienced from me, since I grew up until I completed forty years, any disgrace (خزية), humiliation (ذلّة), lie (كذبة), treachery (خيانة), error in speech, or foolishness in opinion (سفهاً من الرّأي)? Do you imagine that a man could protect himself throughout this entire period by his own power and strength, or by the power and strength of Allah?" And that is what Allah said: See how they strike parables for you, so they have gone astray, and cannot find a way انْظُرْ كَيْفَ ضَرَبُوا لَكَ الْأَمْثالَ فَضَلُّوا فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلاً —meaning, until they can establish blindness against you with a proof, most of their false claims are made clear to you for the realization of their falsehood.

(١١) بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِالسَّاعَةِ ** (11) (No, but they deny the Hour) So their views were limited to the worldly ephemeral things (الحطام الدنيويّة), and they thought that honor (الكرامة) is only through wealth (المال), and they slandered you because of your poverty. وَأَعْتَدْنا لِمَنْ كَذَّبَ بِالسَّاعَةِ سَعِيراً ** (and We have prepared a blaze for those who deny the Hour) a fire of intense burning (شديدة الاسعار).

(١٢) إِذا رَأَتْهُمْ ** (12) When it sees them from a distant place when it is within their sight مِنْ مَكانٍ بَعِيدٍ (from a distant place). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi, he said: [It is] from the distance of a year's journey. سَمِعُوا لَها (they will hear for it) تَغَيُّظاً وَزَفِيراً ** (a raging and a roaring) meaning, the sound of rage (صوت تغيّظ).

(١٣) وَإِذا أُلْقُوا مِنْها مَكاناً ضَيِّقاً مُقَرَّنِينَ (13) And when they are cast into a narrow place of it, chained together, they will call out for perdition there. Al-Qummi said: [It means] shackled (مقيّدين), some of them with others. دَعَوْا هُنالِكَ ثُبُوراً ** (they will call out for perdition there) meaning, destruction (هلاكاً). That is, they will wish for destruction and call upon it.

(١٤) لا تَدْعُوا الْيَوْمَ ثُبُوراً واحِداً ** (14) 'Do not call out for one perdition today, but call out for many perditions!' meaning, that will be said to them. وَادْعُوا ثُبُوراً كَثِيراً ** (but call out for many perditions!') because your torment consists of many types (أنواع كثيرة).

(١٥) قُلْ أَذلِكَ خَيْرٌ أَمْ جَنَّةُ الْخُلْدِ الَّتِي وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ كانَتْ لَهُمْ جَزاءً وَمَصِيراً. (15) Say, 'Is that better, or the Garden of Eternity which the Godwary have been promised, which will be their reward and destination?'

(١٦) لَهُمْ فِيها ما يَشاؤُنَ خالِدِينَ كانَ عَلى رَبِّكَ وَعْداً مَسْؤُلاً ** (16) Therein they will have whatever they wish, abiding [forever]. This is a promise binding upon your Lord, which is liable to be sought. ما يَشاؤُنَ ** (whatever they wish) was a promised thing worthy of being sought and requested, or the people sought it by their saying: رَبَّنا وَآتِنا ما وَعَدْتَنا عَلى رُسُلِكَ ** (Our Lord! Give us what You have promised us through Your apostles) or the angels sought it by their saying: وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ.** (and admit them into the Gardens of Eden).

(١٧) وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ وَما يَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (17) And the day He will muster them and what they worship besides Allah This encompasses every object of worship besides Him. فَيَقُولُ ** (Then He will say)—i.e., to the worshipped ones—وقرء (And it was recited) with the nūn in both [verbs]: أَأَنْتُمْ أَضْلَلْتُمْ عِبادِي هؤُلاءِ أَمْ هُمْ ضَلُّوا السَّبِيلَ (Was it you who misled these servants of Mine, or did they stray from the way themselves?)—due to their failure in sound contemplation (النظر الصحيح) and their turning away from the sincere guide (المرشد النصيح). And this is an interrogative statement of reprimand and rebuke (استفهام تقريع وتبكيت) for the worshippers (العبدة).

(١٨) قالُوا سُبْحانَكَ ** (18) They will say, 'Immaculate are You! (This is said) out of astonishment at what was said to them, because they are either angels and infallible prophets (معصومون) or inanimate objects (جمادات) that are incapable of anything, and (it is) an indication that they are those marked (الموسومون) by His glorification (تسبيحه) and His Oneness (توحيده). So how is it fitting for them to lead His servants astray? Or (it is) a declaration of Allah's transcendence (تنزيهاً) above rivals (الأنداد). ما كانَ يَنْبَغِي لَنا ** It is not proper for us أَنْ نَتَّخِذَ مِنْ دُونِكَ مِنْ أَوْلِياءَ it was not for us to take any guardians besides You. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He recited (نتّخذ) with a dammah on the nūn and a fathah on the khā’. وَلكِنْ مَتَّعْتَهُمْ وَآباءَهُمْ But You provided them and their fathers with comforts (with various kinds of blessings (النّعم) and they became engrossed in desires (الشهوات)) حَتَّى نَسُوا الذِّكْرَ until they forgot the Reminder, (until they became heedless of Your remembrance (ذكرك), and of recalling Your bounties (آلائك), and of reflecting upon Your signs (آياتك)). وَكانُوا قَوْماً بُوراً ** and they were a ruined lot.' (ruined (هالكين)).

(١٩) فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوكُمْ ** (They have certainly denied you) This is a shift (التفات) in address towards the worshippers, presenting an argument and an obligation, assuming the omission of the word 'saying' (القول). And the meaning is that the worshipped ones have certainly denied you بِما تَقُولُونَ ** (in what you say), regarding your saying that they are gods and that these ones led us astray. And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياء), meaning كَذَّبُوكُمْ ** (they have denied you) by their saying: سُبْحانَكَ ما كانَ يَنْبَغِي لَنا فَما ** (Glory be to You! It was not proper for us. So they cannot)—meaning the worshipped ones. And it was read with the tāʾ (بالتاء), addressing the worshippers, [they cannot avert] an averting (صَرْفاً)—repelling the punishment from you—وَلا نَصْراً ** (nor help), so that they might assist you against it. وَمَنْ يَظْلِمْ مِنْكُمْ نُذِقْهُ عَذاباً كَبِيراً ** (And whoever of you commits injustice, We shall make him taste a great punishment), which is the Fire (النار).

(٢٠) وَما أَرْسَلْنا قَبْلَكَ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا إِنَّهُمْ لَيَأْكُلُونَ الطَّعامَ وَيَمْشُونَ فِي الْأَسْواقِ ** (20) We did not send any apostles before you but that they surely ate food and walked in the marketplaces. This is a response to their saying: ما لِهذَا الرَّسُولِ يَأْكُلُ الطَّعامَ وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْواقِ.** (What is the matter with this Apostle that he eats food and walks in the marketplaces?)

In al-Majma', narrated from Ali عليه السلام: that He read يُمَشَّوْنَ (they are made to walk) with a ḍammah on the yā' and a fatḥah on the emphasized shīn, meaning: their needs or the people make them walk (يُمَشِّيهِمْ).

وَجَعَلْنا بَعْضَكُمْ ** O people, لِبَعْضٍ فِتْنَةً ** a trial (ابتلاء). And among that is the trial of the poor by the rich, and the trial of the Messengers by those sent to them, and their showing hostility towards them and harming them. And this is a consolation for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding what they said after their rejection.

أَتَصْبِرُونَ ** This is the reason (علّة) for the making/appointment, meaning: so that We may know which of you is patient, and it is an urging towards patience regarding that by which they were tried.

**وَكانَ رَبُّكَ بَصِيراً ** He is All-seeing of him who is patient and him who is not patient.

(٢١) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ لا يَرْجُونَ لِقاءَنا ** (21) And those who do not expect to meet Us (They do not expect Our meeting) with good, due to their disbelief in the Resurrection (البعث). The root meaning of اللّقاء (meeting) is الوصول (arrival). لَوْ لا ** (Why not/If only) أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْنَا الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (so that) they may inform us of Muhammad's truthfulness, or they become messengers to us, OR we see our Lord so He commands us to believe him and follow him. لَقَدِ اسْتَكْبَرُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ (They have certainly been arrogant in their souls) regarding this matter, وَعَتَوْا (and they defied), meaning they exceeded the limit in injustice (الظلم), عُتُوًّا كَبِيراً ** (with great defiance), reaching the furthest extent of its ranks, since they witnessed the overwhelming miracles (المعجزات القاهرة) yet turned away from them. And they proposed for their wicked souls (لِأَنْفُسهم الخبيثة) that which the aspirations of the holy souls (النفوس القدسيّة) were barred from reaching.

(٢٢) يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ الْمَلائِكَةَ ** (22) The day they see the angels, the angels of death or the angels of torment (العذاب). لا بُشْرى يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُجْرِمِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ حِجْراً مَحْجُوراً ** (No good news that day for the guilty, and they will say: "A forbidden barrier!") They seek refuge from them and ask Allah that He prevent their meeting, and it is from what they used to say upon meeting an enemy or the onset of something disliked (مكروه).

(٢٣) وَقَدِمْنا إِلى ما عَمِلُوا مِنْ عَمَلٍ فَجَعَلْناهُ هَباءً مَنْثُوراً. (23) And We shall advance toward whatever deeds they have done, and turn them into scattered dust.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: Even if their deeds were whiter than the al-Qabati (القباطي), Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will say to them: "Be هَباءً مَنْثُوراً ** (scattered dust)." That is because when the unlawful (الحرام) was presented to them, they took it. And in one narration: they did not abandon it.

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will resurrect a people before whom is a light like the al-Qabati (القباطي). Then He will say to it: "Be هَباءً مَنْثُوراً ** (scattered dust)." Then he said: By Allah, they used to fast and pray, but when something of the unlawful (الحرام) was presented to them, they took it. And when something of the virtue (فضل) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was mentioned to them, they denied it. He said: And the scattered dust (الهباء المنثور) is that which you see entering the house through the small opening (الكوّة) from the rays of the sun.

And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whose deeds these were, and he said: "The deeds of those who hate us and those who hate our followers (شيعتنا)."

(٢٤) أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَيْرٌ مُسْتَقَرًّا ** (24) The inhabitants of paradise will be better lodged that day وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلاً ** and fairer in respect of the resting place.

A place where one settles (يستقر) most of the time for socializing and conversing. A place where one seeks refuge (يؤوى إليه) for rest (للاسترواح).

It is said that this is a metaphorical usage (تجوّز) derived from the place of the midday rest (القيلولة), based on analogy (التشبيه), since there is no sleep in Paradise.

In al-Kafi, in the narrative concerning the questioning in the grave, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), He said: "Then they [the two angels] open a door for him to Paradise, and then they say to him: 'Sleep with a cool eye (قرير العين), the sleep of the youthful, comfortable one, for Allah says: أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَيْرٌ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلاً (The inhabitants of paradise will be better lodged that day and fairer in respect of the resting place).'"

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "It has reached us—and Allah knows best—that when the inhabitants of the Fire are brought forth toward the Fire, before they enter the Fire, it will be said to them: 'Enter into a shade of three columns (ظِلٍّ ذي ثَلثٍ شُعَبٍ) made of the Fire's smoke.' They will assume that it is Paradise. Then they will enter the Fire in groups (أفواجاً), and that will be midday. And the inhabitants of Paradise will approach, enjoying the gifts (التّحف) they desired, until they are given their stations in Paradise at midday. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ ** [until the end of] the Ayah."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "That day will not reach its midpoint until the inhabitants of Paradise approach Paradise and the inhabitants of the Fire approach the Fire."

(٢٥) وَيَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ السَّماءُ ** (And the day the heaven splits) It is تَتَشَقَّقُ. And it was read with the doubling (تَشْدِيد) of the shīn.

**بِالْغَمامِ ** (with the clouds) Due to the rising of the clouds from it (the heaven).

قيل It was said that this is the cloud mentioned in His saying: هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ اللهُ فِي ظُلَلٍ مِنَ الْغَمامِ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ (Do they await anything but that Allah should come to them in the shadows of the clouds, along with the angels)

وَنُزِّلَ الْمَلائِكَةُ تَنْزِيلاً (and the angels are sent down, a [successive] sending down)

وقرء And it was read وَيُنَزِّلُ (and He sends down) from the root الْإِنْزَال (sending down), with الْمَلَائِكَة (the angels) in the accusative case (نَصْب).

قيل It was said: meaning, in those clouds, with the scrolls of deeds.

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): بِالْغَمامِ ** (with the clouds) means the Commander of the Faithful (أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ).

(٢٦) الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْحَقُّ لِلرَّحْمنِ ** (26) The sovereignty on that day will belong truly to the All-beneficent. The established [sovereignty] belongs to Him because every sovereignty will cease on that day, and nothing will remain except His sovereignty وَكانَ يَوْماً عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ عَسِيراً ** and it will be a hard day for the faithless. severe. (٢٧) وَيَوْمَ يَعَضُّ الظَّالِمُ عَلى يَدَيْهِ ** (27) The day when the wrongdoer bites his hands out of excessive regret. Al-Qummi said: [This refers to] the first one (الأوّل) يَقُولُ يا لَيْتَنِي اتَّخَذْتُ مَعَ الرَّسُولِ سَبِيلاً. saying, 'I wish I had followed the Apostle's way! Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: (عليّاً وليّاً) 'Ali as a guardian (وليّاً).

(٢٨) يا وَيْلَتى لَيْتَنِي لَمْ أَتَّخِذْ فُلاناً خَلِيلاً ** (28) 'Woe to me! I wish I had not taken so-and-so for a friend!' He said: meaning the second one (الثاني). (٢٩) لَقَدْ أَضَلَّنِي عَنِ الذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ جاءَنِي ** (29) 'Certainly he led me astray from the Remembrance after it had come to me.' He said: meaning the Wilayah (الولاية). وَكانَ الشَّيْطانُ ** He said: and he is the second one (الثاني) لِلْإِنْسانِ خَذُولاً.** And Satan is a betrayer of man.

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in the Sermon of al-Wasilah (خطبة الوسيلة), he said: "Regarding merits (مناقب), if I were to mention them, the elevation would be great and the listening would be prolonged. But two wretched ones (شقيان) donned it (the position) besides me, and disputed with me concerning that which was not their right, and they rode upon it as misguidance (ضلالة) and believed in it as ignorance (جهالة). That which they returned to is not what they were upon, and wretched is the resting place (مهداً) they prepared for themselves. They curse one another in their abodes, and each one disavows his companion. One says to his intimate companion (قرينه) when they meet: 'I wish there were between me and you the distance of the two Easts (بُعْدَ الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ), for wretched is the companion (الْقَرِينُ).' Then the more wretched one (الأشقى) answers him upon his leap: 'I wish I had not taken you as a friend (خَلِيلاً). Certainly you led me astray عَنِ الذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ جاءَنِي وَكانَ الشَّيْطانُ لِلْإِنْسانِ خَذُولاً ** (from the Remembrance after it had come to me. And Satan is a betrayer of man).' So I am الذِّكْرِ ** (the Remembrance) from which the path went astray, the way (السبيل) from which they deviated, the faith (الايمان) by which they disbelieved, the Quran (القرآن) which they abandoned, the religion (الدين) which they denied, and the Path (الصراط) from which they turned away."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him), in his argumentation against some of the atheists (الزنادقة), he said: "Indeed, Allah concealed the names of those who were deceived, who tempted His creation, and who went astray and led others astray. And He alluded to their names in His saying: **وَيَوْمَ يَعَضُّ الظَّالِمُ عَلى يَدَيْهِ ** (And the day the wrongdoer bites his hands) [the two verses]."

(٣٠) وَقالَ الرَّسُولُ يا رَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِي اتَّخَذُوا هذَا الْقُرْآنَ مَهْجُوراً ** (30) And the Apostle will say, 'My Lord! Indeed my people have taken this Quran as a thing to be forsaken.' By abandoning it and turning away from it. (٣١) وَكَذلِكَ جَعَلْنا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوًّا مِنَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (31) Thus have We appointed for every prophet an enemy from among the guilty. Just as We made them for you, so be patient as they were patient. وَكَفى بِرَبِّكَ هادِياً وَنَصِيراً (And sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and a Helper) for you against them. And a narrative from al-Ihtijaj (الاحتجاج) has already preceded in المقدّمة السادسة (the Sixth Introduction), in which there is a clarification for this Ayah.

**(٣٢) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الْقُرْآنُ ** (32) The faithless say, 'Why has not the Quran been sent down to him all at once?'

Meaning, it was sent down to him as a piece of news (كخبر), in the sense of being informed (أخبر), so that it does not contradict His saying: جُمْلَةً واحِدَةً ** (all at once), all at once, like the three Books. كَذلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِ فُؤادَكَ ** (It is) so that We may strengthen your heart thereby. Meaning, thus We sent it down piecemeal (مفرّقاً), so that by its gradual revelation We may strengthen your heart (فؤادك) for its memorization and understanding. And because when Gabriel brings it down, state after state (حالاً بعد حال), your heart is strengthened thereby. وَرَتَّلْناهُ تَرْتِيلاً (and We have recited it in a measured recitation). And We recited it to you, thing after thing (شيئاً بعد شيء), with deliberation (تؤدة) and slowness (تمهّل), over twenty years.

(٣٣) وَلا يَأْتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ ** (33) And they do not bring you any parable (which) crushes it in its answer, وَأَحْسَنَ تَفْسِيراً ** and with that which is better in clarification (بياناً) and meaning (معنى) than their question. (٣٤) الَّذِينَ يُحْشَرُونَ عَلى وُجُوهِهِمْ إِلى جَهَنَّمَ أُوْلئِكَ شَرٌّ مَكاناً وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً (34) Those who will be mustered on their faces toward hell—those are in a worse plight and further astray from the way. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked how the disbeliever will be mustered on his face on the Day of Resurrection. He said: "Indeed, the One Who made him walk on his two feet is capable of making him walk on his face on the Day of Resurrection."

(٣٥) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ وَجَعَلْنا مَعَهُ أَخاهُ هارُونَ وَزِيراً ** (35) Certainly We gave Moses the Book, and appointed his brother Aaron with him as a minister. (He) supports him in the call (الدّعوة) and the exaltation of the word (الكلمة). (٣٦) فَقُلْنَا اذْهَبا إِلَى الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا ** (36) We said, 'Go to the people who denied Our signs.' Meaning Pharaoh and his people. فَدَمَّرْناهُمْ تَدْمِيراً ** Then We destroyed them utterly. So they went to them, and they denied them both, Then We destroyed them. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): فدمّرناهم (Then We destroyed them) is read with the emphasis of the heavy nūn (النون الثقيلة). And in another narration: فدمّراهم** (Then they both destroyed them). He said: And this is as if it were a command to Moses and Aaron that they should destroy them.

(37) وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ لَمَّا كَذَّبُوا الرُّسُلَ أَغْرَقْناهُمْ ** with the flood (بالطوفان), وَجَعَلْناهُمْ لِلنَّاسِ آيَةً an admonition (عبرة), وَأَعْتَدْنا لِلظَّالِمِينَ عَذاباً أَلِيماً.** (37) (And the people of Noah, when they denied the apostles, We drowned them and made them a sign for mankind, and We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.)

(38) وَعاداً وَثَمُودَ ** And We made عاداً وَثَمُودَ ** also وَأَصْحابَ الرَّسِّ وَقُرُوناً ** and people of ages (اهل اعصار) بَيْنَ ذلِكَ كَثِيراً ** whom none knows except Allah. (38) (and [We destroyed] ‘Ād and Thamūd, and the People of the Well, and many generations between them.)

(39) وَكُلًّا ضَرَبْنا لَهُ الْأَمْثالَ ** We clarified for him the wondrous stories from the narratives of the ancients, as excuses (اعذاراً) and warnings (إنذاراً). When they persisted, they were destroyed, just as He said: وَكُلًّا تَبَّرْنا تَتْبِيراً ** We crushed him utterly (فتّناه تفتيناً). From this comes al-Tibr (التَّبر), which is the fragments of gold and silver. (39) (For each We drew parables, and each We destroyed utterly.)

In al-Ma'ani and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It means] "We shattered utterly (كسّرنا تكسيراً)." Al-Qummi added that he (peace be upon him) said: "It is a word in the Nabataean language (بالنبطيّة)."

In al-Uyun and al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Rida, from his father, from his father, from his father al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon him), he said: Three days before his martyrdom, a man from the nobles of Tamim, named Amr, came to Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, inform me about أَصْحابَ الرَّسِ ** (the People of the Well): in which era were they, where were their dwellings, who was their king, did Allah send a messenger to them or not, and by what means were they destroyed? For I find mention of them in the Book of Allah, the Almighty, but I do not find their story." Ali (peace be upon him) said: "You have asked about a narrative that no one before you has asked me about, and no one after me will narrate it except from me. There is no verse (آية) in the Book of Allah, the Almighty, except that I know it and know its interpretation (تفسيرها), and in what place it was revealed, whether on plain ground (سهل) or mountain (جبل), and at what time, whether night or day. Indeed, here is abundant knowledge (علماً جمّاً)"—and he pointed to his chest—"but its seekers are few. Soon you will regret it if you lose me. "Part of their story, O brother of Tamim, is that they were a people who worshipped a pine tree (شجرة صنوبر) called Shah Darkht (شاه درخت). Yafith ibn Nuh (Japheth son of Noah) had planted it on the bank (شفيرة) of a spring (عين) called Rūshāb (روشاب), which had sprouted the growth (النوح) after the Flood (الطّوفان). "They were only named أَصْحابَ الرَّسِ ** (the People of the Well) because they buried (رسّوا) their Prophet in the earth (في الأرض). This was after Sulayman ibn Dawud (Solomon son of David). "They had twelve villages on the bank of a river called al-Rass (الرسّ) in the lands of the East (بلاد المشرق), and that river was named after them. At that time, there was no river on earth more abundant (اغزر) or sweeter (أعذب) than it, nor were there villages more numerous or more prosperous than theirs. "One of them was named Abān (ابان), the second Ādhar (آذر), the third Day (دي), the fourth Bahman (بهمن), the fifth Isfandār (إسفندار), the sixth Farwardīn (فروردين), the seventh Urdībihisht (أرديبهشت), the eighth Khurdād (خرداد), the ninth Murdād (مرداد), the tenth Tīr (تير), the eleventh Mihr (مهر), and the twelfth Shahriyūr (شهريور). "Their greatest city was Isfandār (إسفندار), where their king resided. He was called Tarkūd ibn Ghābūr ibn Yārish ibn Sādin ibn Namrūd ibn Kan'ān, the Pharaoh of Ibrahim (peace be upon him). The spring (العين) and the pine tree (الصنوبرة) were there. "They had planted a seed (حبّة) from the pollen (طلع) of that pine tree in every village, and the seed grew into a great tree. They forbade the water of the spring and the rivers, neither drinking from them themselves nor allowing their livestock to drink. Whoever did so, they killed him, saying: 'It is the life of our deities (آلهتنا), so no one should diminish its life.' They and their livestock drank from the River al-Rass (نهر الرسّ) upon which their villages stood. "In every month of the year, they held a festival (عيداً) in every village, where its people would gather. They would drape the tree with a silk curtain (كِلّة من حرير) adorned with various images (أنواع الصّور). Then they would bring a sheep and a cow and sacrifice them as an offering (قرباناً) to the tree, lighting fires around it with firewood. When the smoke and odor (قتارها) of those sacrifices rose into the air, preventing them from looking up at the sky, they would fall prostrate (سجّداً) before the tree, weeping and imploring it to be pleased with them. "The Devil (الشيطان) would come and shake its branches and cry out from its trunk with the cry of a child: 'I am pleased with you, My servants, so be cheerful (طيبوا نفساً) and content (قرّوا عيناً).' "They would then raise their heads, drink wine (الخمر), play musical instruments (المعازف), and take the Dast Band (الدّست بند). They would remain in this state for their day and night, and then depart. "The Persians (العجم) only named their months Abān Māh (ابان ماه), Ādhar Māh (آذر ماه), and others, derived from the names of those villages, because their people would say to one another: 'This is the festival of such-and-such a month, and the festival of such-and-such a month.' "When the festival of their greatest village arrived, their young and old would gather. They would erect a pavilion (سرادقاً) of brocade (ديباج) adorned with various images near the pine tree and the spring. It had twelve doors, one door for the people of each village. They would prostrate to the pine tree outside the pavilion and offer sacrifices to it, manifold more than what they offered to the tree in their villages. "Iblis (إبليس) would then come and shake the pine tree violently (تحريكاً شديداً) and speak from its core with a loud voice (كلاماً جهوريّاً), promising them and giving them hopes far greater than what all the devils (الشياطين) had promised and given them hope for. "They would raise their heads from prostration, overwhelmed with such joy and exhilaration that they would neither recover nor speak due to the drinking and music. They would remain in this state for twelve days and nights, corresponding to the number of their festivals throughout the year, and then depart. "When their disbelief in Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and their worship of others besides Him became prolonged, Allah, the Glorified, sent a Prophet to them from the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), from the descendants of Yahūd (يهود) from Ya'qūb (Jacob). He stayed among them for a long time, calling them to the worship of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and to His knowledge and Lordship (ربوبيته), but they did not follow Him. "When he saw the severity of their persistence in error (الغيّ) and misguidance (الضّلال), and their refusal to accept the guidance (الرشد) and success (النجاح) to which he called them, and when the festival of their great village arrived, he said: 'O Lord, Your servants have refused everything except to deny me and disbelieve in You. They have gone forth worshipping a tree that neither benefits nor harms. Therefore, dry up all their trees and show them Your power (قدرتك) and sovereignty (سلطانك).' "The people awoke to find their trees dried up. This terrified them and cut off their hope (قطع بهم). They split into two factions. One faction said: 'This man who claims to be the Messenger of the God of the heaven and the earth to you has bewitched (سحر) your deities (آلهتكم) to turn your faces away from your deities toward his God.' The other faction said: 'No, rather your deities were angered when they saw this man criticizing them, speaking ill of them, and calling you to worship someone else. So, they veiled their beauty and splendor (حسنها وبهائها) so that you might become angry with him and seek vengeance upon him.' "Their consensus was to kill him. They took long lead pipes (أنابيب طوالاً من رصاص) with wide mouths, and lowered them into the bottom (قرار) of the spring, up to the surface of the water, one above the other, like a wick (مثل اليراع). They drained the water from it. Then they dug a deep well with a narrow entrance in its bottom. They lowered their Prophet into it, sealed its mouth with a great rock, and then removed the pipes from the water. "They said: 'We hope now that our deities will be pleased with us when they see that we have killed the one who spoke ill of them and hindered their worship, and we have buried him beneath their great one (كبيرها) so that it may be satisfied with him, and its light and freshness (نضرتها) will return to us as it was.' "They spent the rest of their day hearing the groaning (أنين) of their Prophet, who was saying: 'My Master (سيّدي), You see the narrowness of my place and the severity of my distress (كربي). Have mercy on the weakness of my support (ركني) and the scarcity of my means (حيلتي). Hasten the taking of my soul (قبض روحي) and do not delay the answer to my supplication (دعوتي),' until he died. "Then Allah, the Almighty, said to Gabriel: 'O Gabriel, do these servants of Mine, whom My forbearance (حلمي) has deceived, who felt safe from My planning (مكري), worshipped others besides Me, and killed My Messenger, think that they can withstand My wrath (غضبي) and escape My dominion (سلطاني)? How can they, when I am the Avenger (المنتقم) against those who disobey Me and do not fear My punishment (عقابي)? I swear by My might (عزّتي) that I shall make them an admonition (عبرة) and a deterrent (نكالاً) for all the worlds.' "While they were at their festival, they were suddenly struck by a violent, intensely red wind (ريح عاصفة شديدة الحمرة). They became bewildered and terrified by it, huddling close to one another. Then the earth beneath them turned into burning sulfur stone (حجر كبريت تتوقّد). A black cloud overshadowed them and cast down upon them, like a dome (كالقبّة), blazing embers (جمراً يلتهب). Their bodies melted away just as lead melts in fire. We seek refuge in Allah, exalted be His remembrance, from His wrath and the descent of His vengeance (نقمته). There is no might and no power except with Allah, the Most High, the Most Great."

And al-Qummi said: al-Rass (الرَّسِ ) is a river in the region of Azerbaijan (آذربايجان).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Some women entered upon him, and one of them asked him about tribadism (السَّحق). He said: "Its prescribed punishment (حدّها) is the prescribed punishment of the adulterer (الزاني)." The woman said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, did not mention that in the Quran." He said: "Yes, He did." She asked: "And where is it?" He replied: "They are أَصْحابَ الرَّسِ. (the People of the Well)."

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), said: A woman entered upon Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) with her maidservant (مولاة لها) and asked: "What do you say about those women who are with those women (اللّواتي مع اللّواتي)?" He said: "They will be in the Fire. When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, they will be brought and dressed in a cloak (جلباباً) of fire, boots (خفّين) of fire, and a veil (قناعاً) of fire. Pillars (اعمدة) of fire will be inserted into their insides (اجوافهنّ) and their private parts (فروجهنّ), and they will be cast into the Fire." She said: "This is not in the Book of Allah." He said: "Yes, it is." She asked: "Where is it?" He replied: "His saying: وَعاداً وَثَمُودَ وَأَصْحابَ الرَّسِ ** (and ‘Ād and Thamūd, and the People of the Well). They are the Rassiyyāt (الرسّيّات)."

In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): The tribadism (سحق النساء) of women was among أَصْحابَ الرَّسِ ** (the People of the Well). And in another wording: Their women were practitioners of tribadism (سحّاقات).

(40) **وَلَقَدْ أَتَوْا ** (And certainly they have come upon the town which was rained upon with the evil rain.) means Quraysh, who passed repeatedly during their trade journeys to Syria عَلَى الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي أُمْطِرَتْ مَطَرَ السَّوْءِ (upon the town which was rained upon with the evil rain).

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: As for الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي أُمْطِرَتْ مَطَرَ السَّوْءِ ** (the town which was rained upon with the evil rain), it is Sadūm, the town of the people of Lot. Allah rained upon them stones of sijjīl (سِجِّيل), meaning: [stones] of clay (طِين). أَفَلَمْ يَكُونُوا يَرَوْنَها ** (Do they not see it?) during their repeated passing, so that they might take heed from the traces of Allah's punishment that they see therein? بَلْ كانُوا لا يَرْجُونَ نُشُوراً ** (Rather they do not expect a resurrection.) Rather, they were disbelievers who did not anticipate a resurrection (نُشُور) nor an outcome (عَاقِبَة). Therefore, they did not look [with reflection] nor did they take heed, so they passed by it just as their riding beasts passed by it.

(٤١) وَإِذا رَأَوْكَ إِنْ يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُواً ** (41) And when they see you, they take you only for a mockery: They do not take you except as an object of mockery (موضع هزءٍ). أَهذَا الَّذِي بَعَثَ اللهُ رَسُولاً ** 'Is this the one whom Allah has sent as an apostle?' meaning, they say that mockingly (تهكّماً) and scornfully (استهزاء). (٤٢) إِنْ كادَ ** (42) 'He had almost led us astray from our gods, had we not stood fast by them.' Soon they will know, when they see the punishment, who is further astray from the way. Indeed, he almost. لَيُضِلُّنا عَنْ آلِهَتِنا ** to turn us away from their worship due to his excessive effort (فرط اجتهاده) in calling to Monotheism (التوحيد) and the abundance of what he presents, which leads the mind to believe they are proofs (حجج) and miracles (معجزات). لَوْ لا أَنْ صَبَرْنا عَلَيْها ** we remained firm upon them and held fast to their worship. وَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوْنَ الْعَذابَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً In this is a threat (وعيد) and an indication that He will not neglect them, even if He grants them respite.

(٤٣) أَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلهَهُ هَواهُ ** (43) Have you seen him who takes his desire to be his god? by obeying it and building his religion upon it. He does not listen to an argument (حجّة) nor does he perceive a proof (دليلاً). أَفَأَنْتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ وَكِيلاً ** Will you then be a guardian over him? a guardian (حفيظاً) preventing him from polytheism (الشرك) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي) while this is his state. Thus, the first interrogation is for affirmation (التّقرير) and astonishment (التعجّب), and the second is for denial (الإنكار).

(٤٤) أَمْ تَحْسَبُ ** Rather, do you suppose أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ ** so that the signs (الآيات) and proofs (الحجج) would benefit them, and you would worry about their affair and hope for their faith (إيمانهم)? أَمْ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَالْأَنْعامِ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً (25:44) (Do you suppose that most of them hear or apply reason? They are merely like cattle; rather, they are further astray from the way.)

And this (state of theirs) is a stronger condemnation than what preceded it, such that it deserved the shift (الإضراب) from that (previous state) to this (current state).

And the specification of 'most of them' (الأكثر) is because there were some among them who believed, and some among them who understood the truth (الحق) but stubbornly denied it out of arrogance (استكباراً) and fear for their leadership (الرياسة).

إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَالْأَنْعامِ ** in their lack of benefit from the signs (الآيات) striking their ears, and their lack of reflection upon the proofs (الدّلائل) and miracles (المعجزات) they witnessed.

بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً ** than the cattle (الأنعام), because they submit to the one who tends to them, and they distinguish between the one who treats them kindly and the one who treats them badly, and they seek what benefits them and avoid what harms them. But these (people) do not submit to their Lord, nor do they recognize the kindness (إحسان) of the All-beneficent (الرحمن) from the malice (إساءة) of Satan (الشيطان), nor do they seek the reward (الثواب) which is the greatest of benefits, nor do they guard against the punishment (العقاب) which is the severest of harms. And because (the cattle), even if they do not believe in truth (حقّاً) and do not earn good (خيراً), they do not believe in falsehood (باطلاً) and do not earn evil (شرّاً), contrary to these (people). And because their ignorance (جهالتها) does not harm anyone, while the ignorance (جهالة) of these (people) leads to the stirring up of tribulations (الفتن) and turning people away from the truth (الحق). And because they (the cattle) are not enabled to achieve perfection (الكمال), there is no shortcoming (تقصير) or blame (ذمّ) upon them, while these (people) are neglectful (مقصّرون) and deserve the greatest punishment (العقاب) for their neglect (تقصيرهم).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Quraysh, and that was because livelihood (المعاش) became difficult for them, so they left Mecca (مكّة) and dispersed. And a man would, when he saw a beautiful tree or a beautiful stone, desire it and worship it. And they would slaughter livestock (النعم) for it and smear them with blood, and call them Sa'd Rock (سعد صخرة). And when a disease (داء) afflicted them in their camels (إبلهم) and sheep (أغنامهم), they would come to the rock (الصخرة) and rub the sheep and camels against it. Then a man from the Arabs came with his camels, intending to rub his camels against the rock (الصخرة) and seek blessing (البركة) from it. But his camels bolted and scattered. So the man said:

"I came to Sa'd (سعد) so that it might gather our scattered group, but Sa'd (سعد) scattered us. So we are not from Sa'd (سعد), and Sakhr (صخر) is nothing but a rock (صخرة) deposited from the earth; it guides neither to error (غيّ) nor to right guidance (رشد)."

And an Arab man passed by it while a fox (الثعلب) was urinating on it. So he said:

وربّ يبول الثعلبان برأسه

لقد ذلّ من بالت عليه الثعالب

And a lord (ربّ) upon whose head the two foxes (الثعلبان) urinate,

Truly, he is disgraced upon whom the foxes (الثعالب) urinate.

(٤٥) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلى رَبِّكَ ** (45) Have you not regarded your Lord Have you not looked at His handiwork (صنعه)? كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّلَ ** how He extends the shade? How He spreads it out?

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He said: الظِّلَ ** (the shade) is what is between the rising of the dawn (طلوع الفجر) and the rising of the sun (طلوع الشمس). It is said that this is the most pleasant of states, for pure darkness repels the nature (الطبع) and obstructs the sight (النظر), and the sun's rays heat the air and dazzle the vision (البصر). For this reason, He described Paradise with it, saying: وَظِلٍّ مَمْدُودٍ (and extended shade).

وَلَوْ شاءَ لَجَعَلَهُ ساكِناً (And had He wished, He would have made it stationary)—meaning fixed (ثابتاً) from dwelling (السّكنى), or non-receding (غير متقلّص) from stillness (السكون), by making the sun remain fixed in one position. ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا الشَّمْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلاً ** Then We made the sun a pointer to it. For it does not become apparent to the senses (للحسّ) until it rises and its light falls upon some of the bodies (الاجرام). Were it not for the sun, the shade (الظّلّ) would not be known, nor would it vary except due to its movement (حركتها).

(٤٦) ثُمَّ قَبَضْناهُ إِلَيْنا ** (46) Then We withdraw it toward Ourselves meaning, We removed it by causing the ray (الشّعاع) to fall upon its location. Since He expressed its bringing forth (احداثه) through the extending (المدّ), meaning facilitation (التّيسير), He expressed its removal (إزالته) through the withdrawing (القبض) toward Himself, which is in the meaning of restraining (الكفّ); a gentle (قَبْضاً يَسِيراً) withdrawing, little by little, corresponding to how the sun rises, so that the welfare (مصالح) of the universe (الكون) may be regulated thereby, and so that countless benefits (منافع) for creation (الخلق) may be attained through it.

(٤٧) وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ لِباساً ** (47) It is He who made the night a covering for you, He likened its darkness to clothing (اللّباس) in its covering (ستره). وَالنَّوْمَ سُباتاً ** and sleep a rest, a rest (راحة) for the bodies by cutting off (بقطع) preoccupations (المشاغل). And the origin of al-sabat (السّبت) is cutting off (القطع). وَجَعَلَ النَّهارَ نُشُوراً ** and made the day a resurrection. possessing nushūr (نُشُور), meaning a spreading out (انتشار) in which people spread out. And in it is an indication that sleep (النوم) and wakefulness (اليقظة) are a model (أنموذج) for death (الموت) and resurrection (النشور). And in the Prophetic Hadith: "As you sleep, you die, and as you wake up, you are resurrected."

(٤٨) وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ الرِّياحَ بُشْراً ** (48) And He it is Who sends the winds as glad tidings meaning, spreading the clouds (ناشرات للسّحاب), or bringing glad tidings (مبشّرات), according to the different readings (القرّاء), as passed in Sūrat al-A‘rāf, بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَحْمَتِهِ meaning: before the rain (المطر). وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً طَهُوراً ** and We send down from the sky pure water purifying (مطهّراً), or highly effective in purity (بليغاً في الطهارة). He described it thus to indicate the blessing (النّعمة) therein and to complete the favor (المنّة) in what follows it, for pure water (الماء الطّهور) is more pleasant (أهنأ) and beneficial (أنفع) than that which is mixed with something that removes its purity (طهوريته).

(٤٩) لِنُحْيِيَ بِهِ بَلْدَةً مَيْتاً ** (49) That We may revive thereby a dead land (We revive it) with vegetation. And (مَيْتاً) 'dead' is mentioned (in the masculine form) because (البلدة) 'the land' has the meaning of (البلد) 'the country/place' (which is masculine). وَ نُسْقِيَهُ مِمَّا خَلَقْنا أَنْعاماً وَ أَناسِيَّ كَثِيراً. and provide it as drink for many of the cattle and humans We have created. (٥٠) وَ لَقَدْ صَرَّفْناهُ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (50) And We have certainly distributed it among them It is said: We distributed this saying (القول) among people in the Quran and other books, or (We distributed) the rain among them in different lands (البلدان المختلفة), at varying times (الأوقات المتغايرة), and with differing qualities (الصفات المتفاوتة), such as heavy rain (وابل), light drizzle (طل), and others. In al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "Not a single day has passed over the people of the world since Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created it, except that the sky rained therein. And Allah places that (rain) wherever He wills," لِيَذَّكَّرُوا (meaning) that they may reflect (ليتفكّروا), recognize the perfection of (His) Power (كمال القدرة) and the true nature of the blessing (حقّ النعمة) therein, fulfill His gratitude (بشكره), and take heed (ويعتبروا) by its distribution away from them and towards them. فَأَبى أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُوراً ** Yet most people refuse to be anything but ingrates. (They refuse) except ingratitude for the blessing (كفران النعمة), little concern for it (قلّة الاكتراث لها), and denial of it (وجحودها) by saying: "We were rained upon by such-and-such a star (نبؤ كذا)," without seeing it as coming from Allah, and by making the celestial lights (الأنوار) subservient intermediaries (وسائط مسخّرات).

(٥١) **وَلَوْ شِئْنا لَبَعَثْنا فِي كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍ نَذِيراً ** (51) Had We wished, We would have surely sent a warner to every town.

A prophet who warns its people, so that the burdens of prophethood would be lightened for you. But We confined the matter to you, out of reverence for you, and magnification of your status, and preferring you over the rest of the Messengers. So respond to that with steadfastness and diligence in the call (الدّعوة) and manifesting the truth.

فَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ (٥٢) (52) So do not obey the faithless, concerning what they want you to do, which is an incitement for him and for the believers, وَجاهِدْهُمْ بِهِ (and strive against them with it) (with) the Quran, or that he leaves their obedience, جِهاداً كَبِيراً (a great striving), meaning that they strive hard to invalidate your truth, so confront them with striving hard in opposing them and removing their falsehood, for striving against the foolish (السّفهاء) with proofs (الحجج) is greater than striving against the enemies (الأعداء) with the sword (السّيف).

(٥٣) وَهُوَ الَّذِي مَرَجَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ ** (And it is He who has merged the two seas) He let them loose, passing by each other, contiguous, such that they do not mix, derived from (مَرَجَ دَابَتَّهُ) (letting his animal loose) when He sets it free. هذا عَذْبٌ فُراتٌ ** (this one palatable and sweet) extremely sweet (بليغ العذوبة). وَهذا مِلْحٌ أُجاجٌ ** (and this one salty and bitter) extremely salty (بليغ الملوحة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from them both (peace be upon them): Indeed, Allah, majestic and mighty is He, presented our wilayah (ولايتنا) (guardianship/authority) to the waters. So, whatever accepted our wilayah became sweet and pleasant, and whatever rejected our wilayah, Allah made it bitter and salty and bitter (ملحاً أجاجاً).

وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَهُما بَرْزَخاً وَحِجْراً مَحْجُوراً (and set a barrier between them, and an insurmountable impediment.) a barrier (حاجزاً) from His power, and an insurmountable impediment (حِجْراً مَحْجُوراً). It was said (that this means) an extreme repulsion (تنافراً بليغاً) or a defined boundary (حدّاً محدوداً). And that is like the Tigris (دجلة) when it enters the sea, splitting it, and flows through its midst for leagues (فراسخ) without its taste changing.

And al-Qummi says: It is absolutely forbidden (حراماً محرّماً) for one of them to change the taste of the other.

(٥٤) وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ الْماءِ بَشَراً ** (54) And it is He who created man from water It was said: meaning the water with which He kneaded the clay (طينة) of Adam (peace be upon him), then He made it a part of the material (مادّة) of mankind so that it might gather, propagate, and easily accept forms (الاشكال). Or (it means) the sperm-drop (النّطفة). فَجَعَلَهُ نَسَباً وَصِهْراً ** and made him [related by] lineage and marriage. So He divided him into two sections: those possessing lineage (ذوي نسب), meaning males to whom lineage is attributed; and those possessing affinity by marriage (ذوات صهر), meaning females through whom marriage affinity (يصاهر بهنّ) is established. وَكانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيراً ** Your Lord is indeed Omnipotent. In that He created from a single material (مادّة واحدة) a human being possessing different organs and divergent temperaments (طباع متباعدة), and made him two opposing sections.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He (the Imam) was asked about this verse, and He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created Adam from fresh water (الماء العذب) and created his wife from his essence (سنخه). So He fashioned her from the lowest of his ribs. By means of that rib, a cause (سبب) and lineage (نسب) flowed between them. Then He married her to him, and by means of that, affinity by marriage (صهر) flowed between them. That is His saying: نَسَباً وَصِهْراً ** (lineage and marriage). So, the lineage (النسب) is what is due to the men, and the affinity by marriage (الصهر) is what is due to the women."

In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Sirin: It was revealed concerning the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام. He (the Prophet) married Fatimah to Ali, who was his paternal cousin, and He married his daughter (to him), so she became نَسَباً وَصِهْراً (lineage and marriage).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both), He said: "Behold! I am specified in the Quran with names. Beware lest you be overcome by them and thus go astray in your religion. I am al-Sihr (the affinity by marriage). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ الْماءِ بَشَراً فَجَعَلَهُ نَسَباً وَصِهْراً (And it is He who created man from water and made him [related by] lineage and marriage)."

In al-Amali, with its chain of transmission leading to Anas ibn Malik, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: I said to Him: "O Messenger of Allah, is Ali your brother?" He said: "Yes, Ali is my brother." I said: "O Messenger of Allah, describe to me how Ali is your brother." He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created water beneath the Throne three thousand years before He created Adam, and He housed it in a green pearl (لؤلؤة خضراء) within the depths of His knowledge until He created Adam. When He created Adam, He transferred that water from the pearl and caused it to flow in the loins (صلب) of Adam until Allah the Almighty took his soul. Then He transferred it to the loins of Seth (شيث). That water continued to be transferred from back to back until it reached Abd al-Muttalib. Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, split it into two halves. One half went into my father, Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib, and the other half went into Abu Talib. So, I am from one half of the water, and Ali is from the other half. Thus, Ali is my brother in this world and the Hereafter." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited: وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ الْماءِ بَشَراً (And it is He who created man from water) [the rest of] the verse.

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created a hidden white sperm-drop (نطفة بيضاء مكنونة), and He transferred it from loin to loin until the sperm-drop was transferred to the loins of Abd al-Muttalib. Then He made it two halves. One half went into Abdullah, and the other half went into Abu Talib. So, I am from Abdullah, and Ali is from Abu Talib. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ (And it is He who created) [the rest of] the verse."

(٥٥) وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَنْفَعُهُمْ وَلا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَكانَ الْكافِرُ عَلى رَبِّهِ ظَهِيراً ** (They worship besides Allah what neither benefits them nor harms them, and the faithless is a backer against his Lord.) He supports (يظاهر) the Devil in enmity and polytheism (الشرك).

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: "Its interpretation is in the inner meaning (بطن) of the Quran: 'Ali is his Lord (ربّه) in terms of guardianship (الولاية), and the Lord (الربّ) is the Creator (الخالق) Who cannot be described."

I say: It means that the Lord (الربّ) absolutely (على الإطلاق), the One not restricted by guardianship (الولاية), is Allah, the Creator (الخالق), exalted be His remembrance.

And al-Qummi [said that] a human may be called a lord (ربّاً), as in His saying, the Almighty: اذْكُرْنِي عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ ** (Mention me to your master.) And every owner (مالك) of a thing is called its lord (ربّه). And regarding His saying, the Almighty: وَكانَ الْكافِرُ عَلى رَبِّهِ ظَهِيراً (and the faithless is a backer against his Lord), he said: The second الْكافِرُ ** (faithless one) was 'Ali, Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), ظَهِيراً. (a backer/supporter).

(56) وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً (56) We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and as a warner. (as a bearer of good news) for the believers (للمؤمنين), (and as a warner) for the disbelievers (للكافرين). (57) قُلْ ما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِلَّا مَنْ شاءَ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ إِلى رَبِّهِ سَبِيلاً (57) Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it, except that whoever wishes may take the path toward his Lord.' (Say, 'I do not ask you for it) meaning, for the conveyance of the message (تبليغ الرسالة), which is indicated by (the preceding verse) إِلَّا مُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً, (any reward, except whoever wishes) meaning, except the action (فعل) of whoever wishes (مَنْ شاءَ) أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ إِلى رَبِّهِ سَبِيلاً (to take a path toward his Lord)—that is, that he draws near to Him (يتقرّب إليه) and seeks closeness (الزّلفى) with Him through faith (بالإيمان) and obedience (والطاعة). So, He depicted that (act of taking the path) in the form of a reward (الأجر) from the perspective that it is the intended goal of His action, and He made it an exception from it (the reward) in order to cut off the suspicion of greed (الطّمع) and to demonstrate the utmost degree of compassion (الشفقة).

(٥٨) وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى الْحَيِّ الَّذِي لا يَمُوتُ ** (58) And rely on the Living One who does not die in seeking His sufficiency against their evils and His enrichment from their rewards, for He is the One truly deserving that reliance be placed upon Him, unlike the living ones who die, for when they die, whoever relied upon them is lost. وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِهِ ** And glorify Him with His praise and purify Him from attributes of deficiency, praising Him with attributes of perfection, seeking increased favor by thanking Him for His previous blessings. وَكَفى بِهِ بِذُنُوبِ عِبادِهِ خَبِيراً ** And sufficient is He as an all-aware of the sins of His servants** what appeared of them and what was hidden, so it is not incumbent upon you whether they believe or disbelieve.

(٥٩) **الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ** (59) He who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days, and then settled on the Throne.

The discussion concerning this has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf. And perhaps its mention here is for increased confirmation, because He is worthy of reliance (التوكل) since He is the Creator of all things and the Disposer therein. And it is an encouragement toward steadfastness (الثبات) and deliberation (التأنّي) in the matter, for indeed, He, the Exalted, despite the perfection of His power and the swiftness of the execution of His command, created things with calmness (تؤدة) and gradualness (تدرّج). And this meaning has already passed in their sayings (peace be upon them).

الرَّحْمنُ (al-Rahman) is the predicate (خبر) of الَّذِي (He Who) if you consider it to be the subject (مبتدأ), or it is the predicate of an omitted word if you consider it to be an attribute (صفة) of الْحَيُّ (the Living One) or a substitute (بدل) for the pronoun implicit (المستكن) in اسْتَوى (He settled).

فَسْئَلْ بِهِ خَبِيراً فَسْئَلْ ** Ask concerning what was mentioned regarding the creation and the settling (الاستواء), or concerning whether He is al-Rahman (the All-beneficent).

In al-Majma', it is narrated: The Jews spoke about the beginning of the creation of things contrary to what Allah, the Exalted, informed, so He, the Glorified, said: فَسْئَلْ بِهِ خَبِيراً (So ask about Him one who is well-informed). And asking (السؤال), just as it is transitive using عن (concerning) because it implies the meaning of investigation (التفتيش), it is also transitive using باء (بِـ) because it implies the meaning of concern (الاعتناء). And it is permissible for it (بِهِ) to be related to (صلة) خَبِيراً (one who is well-informed). And al-Khabir (the well-informed one) is Allah, the Glorified, or Gabriel (جبرئيل), or one whom you find in the preceding scriptures (الكتب المتقدّمة) who will confirm it for you. Thus it was said.

أقولُ : I say: And it is probable that what is meant by it (بِهِ) are the preceding Messengers (الرسل المتقدّمة), and thus the questioning (السؤال) would be in the realm of spirits (عالم الأرواح). Like His saying, the Exalted: وَسْئَلْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنا أَجَعَلْنا مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمنِ آلِهَةً يُعْبَدُونَ ** (And ask those of Our apostles We sent before you, whether We have set up gods besides the All-beneficent to be worshipped.) And it was said that the pronoun (الضمير) refers to al-Rahman (the All-beneficent), and the meaning is: If they deny its application (إطلاقه) to Allah, then ask about Him (عنه) one who can inform you from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), so that you may know the occurrence of what is synonymous with it (ما يرادفه) in their scriptures.

(٦٠) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اسْجُدُوا لِلرَّحْمنِ قالُوا وَمَا الرَّحْمنُ ** (60) When they are told, 'Prostrate before the All-beneficent,' they say, 'What is the All-beneficent?' It was said because they did not apply it [the term] to Allah, or because they thought that He intended by it someone other than Him, the Exalted. Al-Qummi said that His answer is: الرَّحْمنُ عَلَّمَ الْقُرْآنَ خَلَقَ الْإِنْسانَ عَلَّمَهُ الْبَيانَ (The All-beneficent (1) has taught the Quran. (2) He created man, (3) taught him articulate speech. (4)) أَ نَسْجُدُ لِما تَأْمُرُنا (Shall we prostrate to what you command us?) And it was recited with the yāʾ (بالياء) [as wa zādahum], وَزادَهُمْ نُفُوراً (and it increased them in aversion) from faith (عن الإيمان), meaning the command to prostrate to the All-beneficent.

(61) تَبارَكَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ فِي السَّماءِ بُرُوجاً ** (61) Blessed is He who placed constellations in the sky, meaning the twelve constellations (البروج الاثني عشر), and their explanation has already preceded in Surat al-Hijr. وَجَعَلَ فِيها سِراجاً ** and placed in it a lamp, meaning the sun, due to His saying: وَجَعَلَ الشَّمْسَ سِراجاً ** (and He made the sun a lamp). And it was recited as سُرُجاً (lamps) with two ḍammahs, which would include the large stars (الكواكب الكبار).

In al-Jawami', narrated from them (peace be upon them): "Do not recite سُرُجاً (lamps), rather it is سِراجاً ** (a lamp), and it is the sun." وَقَمَراً مُنِيراً ** and a luminous moon. shining brightly (مضيئاً) by night.

In al-Ihlilajah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), in a discourse of His: وَجَعَلَ فِيها سِراجاً وَقَمَراً مُنِيراً ** (and placed in it a lamp and a luminous moon). They swim (يسبحان) in an orbit (فلك) that revolves with them, constantly moving (دائبين). He makes them rise at one time and set at another, so that the number of days, months, and years may be known, and what is initiated of summer, spring, winter, and autumn—different seasons (أزمنة مختلفة) due to the variation of night and day.

(62) وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ خِلْفَةً ** (And He is the One Who made the night and the day a succession) Each of them succeeds the other by standing in its place regarding what ought to be done therein. لِمَنْ أَرادَ أَنْ يَذَّكَّرَ ** (for him who desires to remember) And it was read with takhfif (lightening the dhāl), or desires gratitude (شُكُوراً).

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Everything that you missed during the night, make it up during the day." Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said—and He recited this Ayah—then He said: "Meaning that the man makes up what he missed during the night in the day, and what he missed during the day in the night."

And in al-Tahdhib and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), is that which is close to it. And al-Qummi added: "And it is from the hidden secret (سرّ) of the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد)."

(٦٣) وَعِبادُ الرَّحْمنِ الَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ هَوْناً. (63) And the servants of the All-beneficent are those who walk on the earth humbly.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He is the man who walks according to his nature (سجيّته) upon which he was created, neither being pretentious (لا يتكلّف) nor arrogant (ولا يتبختر).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He said regarding this verse that the Imams walk on the earth humbly (يَمْشُونَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ هَوْناً) out of fear of their enemy.

And from al-Kazim, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: They are the Imams (peace be upon them); they practice caution (يتّقون) in their walking.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: They are the Successors (الأوصياء), out of fear of their enemy. وَإِذا خاطَبَهُمُ الْجاهِلُونَ قالُوا سَلاماً (And when the ignorant address them, they say, 'Peace!'), meaning: a peaceful withdrawal from you and a leaving of you. There is neither good nor evil between us.

(٦٤) وَالَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ سُجَّداً وَقِياماً ** (64) And those who spend the night prostrating and standing before their Lord, [This is] in the prayer (الصلاة). The specification of spending the night (البيتوتة) is because worship at night is purer (أخلص) and further removed from showing off (الرياء). (٦٥) وَالَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا اصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذابَ جَهَنَّمَ إِنَّ عَذابَها كانَ غَراماً ** (65) And those who say, 'Our Lord! Turn away from us the punishment of hell. Indeed, its punishment is a lasting affliction. [It means] inseparable (لازماً). And from this [root] is the word الغريم (creditor/debtor) due to his clinging (ملازمته). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: [It means] clinging (ملازماً), it does not depart. I say: This is an indication that, despite their good conduct (مخالقتهم) with creation and their striving (اجتهادهم) in the worship of the Truth (الحقّ), they are fearful (وجلون) of the punishment, imploring Allah to turn it away from them, due to their not relying upon their deeds (أعمالهم) and their lack of confidence in the continuation of their states (أحوالهم).

(٦٦) إِنَّها ساءَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقاماً ** (66) Indeed it is a bad abode and station. The two sentences potentially represent a narration (الحكاية) or an initiation (الابتداء) from Allah. (٦٧) وَالَّذِينَ إِذا أَنْفَقُوا لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا ** (67) And those who, when they spend, are neither extravagant nor stingy, And it was read (وقرء) with a kasrah on the tā’ (بكسر التاء) from (اقتر). وَكانَ بَيْنَ ذلِكَ قَواماً. and are moderate in between these. Al-Qummi (said): Al-Isrāf (الإسراف) is spending in disobedience (المعصية) in what is not rightful (في غير حقّ). وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا ** means they were not stingy (لم يبخلوا) regarding the right of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجل). And al-Qawām (القوام) is justice (العدل) and spending in what Allah has commanded. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever gives in what is not rightful has committed extravagance (أسرف), and whoever withholds what is rightful has committed stinginess (قتر)." And from Ali عليه السلام: "There is no extravagance (سرف) in what is eaten and drunk, even if it is abundant." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Extravagance (الإسراف) is only in what ruins wealth (المال) and harms the body (البدن)." It was asked: "Then what is stinginess (الإقتار)?" He said: "Eating bread and salt while you are able to afford something else." It was asked: "Then what is moderation (القصد)?" He said: "Bread, meat, milk, vinegar, and clarified butter (السَّمن); sometimes this and sometimes that." And from him عليه السلام: He recited this verse, then took a handful (قبضة) of pebbles (حصى) and clenched it in His hand, saying: "This is the stinginess (الإقتار) which Allah mentioned in His Book." Then He took another handful, and released His entire palm, saying: "This is the extravagance (الإسراف)." Then He took another handful, and released some of it and held back some of it, and said: "This is the moderation (القوام)."

(٦٨) وَالَّذِينَ لا يَدْعُونَ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ وَلا يَقْتُلُونَ النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللهُ ** (68) And those who do not invoke another god besides Allah, and do not kill a soul which Allah has forbidden meaning, He forbade it in the sense that He forbade its killing, إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَلا يَزْنُونَ وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ يَلْقَ أَثاماً ** except by right, nor commit adultery. And whoever does that shall encounter its penalty. the recompense of sin (جَزَاءَ إِثْمٍ).

(٦٩) يُضاعَفْ لَهُ الْعَذابُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَيَخْلُدْ فِيهِ مُهاناً ** (69) The punishment shall be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he shall abide in it in disgrace. And يُضاعَفْ was read with the raf' (الرفع), and by omitting the alif and using tashdid (التّشديد), in both the nominative (مرفوعاً) and the jussive (مجزوماً). And يَخْلُدْ follows it in the nominative (الرفع) and the jussive (الجزم). Al-Qummi (said): Athām (اثام) is a valley among the valleys of Hell (جهنّم), made of molten copper (صفر مذاب). Before it is a sharp intensity (حدّة) in Hell. In it will be those who worshipped other than Allah, and those who killed النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللهُ ** (the soul which Allah has forbidden). And in it will be the adulterers (الزّناة), and the punishment shall be doubled for them therein.

(٧٠) إِلَّا مَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلاً صالِحاً فَأُوْلئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ اللهُ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ حَسَناتٍ وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً (70) except him who repents, attains faith, and acts righteously. For such, Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

In al-Amali, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَأُوْلئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ اللهُ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ حَسَناتٍ (For such, Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds). He said: The sinning believer (المؤمن المذنب) will be brought forth on the Day of Resurrection until he is made to stand at the station of reckoning (موقف الحساب). Allah, the Exalted, will be the One Who undertakes his reckoning; He will not allow anyone among the people to know of his reckoning. He will make him aware of his sins until, when he acknowledges his evil deeds (سيّئاته), Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will say to the scribes (الكتبة): "Replace them with good deeds (حسنات) and reveal them to the people." The people will then say: "This servant did not have a single evil deed." Then Allah will command that he be taken to Paradise. This is the interpretation (تأويل) of the Ayah, and it pertains specifically to the sinners among our followers (شيعتنا).

And narrated from al-Rida, from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Our love, the love of the People of the House (أهل البيت), expiates sins (يكفّر الذّنوب) and multiplies good deeds (يضاعف الحسنات). Indeed, Allah takes responsibility for the injustices (مظالم العباد) owed by those who love us, the People of the House, except for what they committed by harming and oppressing believers. Then He says to the evil deeds (السيّئات): 'Be good deeds (حسنات).'"

And in al-Uyun, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "When the Day of Resurrection comes, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will manifest Himself to His believing servant and make him stand before his sins, sin by sin. Then He will forgive him. Allah will not allow any close angel (ملكاً مقرّباً) or sent prophet (نبيّاً مرسلاً) to know of this, and He will conceal from him what he dislikes anyone to know. Then He will say to his evil deeds (سيّئاته): 'Be good deeds (حسنات).'"
