And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: When the Day of Resurrection comes, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will make the believer stand before Him and present his deeds to him. He will look at his scroll (صحيفته), and the first thing he sees will be his evil deeds (سيّئاته). His color will change because of this, and his joints (فرائصه) will tremble. Then his good deeds (حسناته) will be presented to him, and his soul will rejoice because of that. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will say: "Replace his evil deeds with good deeds and reveal them to the people." So Allah will replace them for them, and the people will say: "Did these people not have a single evil deed?" This is His saying, the Almighty: يُبَدِّلُ اللهُ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ حَسَناتٍ (Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds). And the narrations (الأخبار) concerning this meaning are numerous.

And in the narration (حديث) of Abu Ishaq al-Laythi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), which was mentioned concerning the clay (طينة) of the believer and the clay of the disbeliever, the meaning of which is that Allah, the Glorified, commands on the Day of Resurrection that the good deeds (حسنات) of our enemies be taken and returned to our followers (شيعتنا), and that the evil deeds (سيّئات) of those who love us (مُحِبِّينَا) be taken and returned to those who hate us (مبغضينا). He said: This is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: فَأُوْلئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ اللهُ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ حَسَناتٍ (For such, Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds). Allah replaces the evil deeds of our followers with good deeds, and Allah replaces the good deeds of our enemies with evil deeds.

And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: There is no group of people who sit remembering Allah (يذكرون الله) except that a caller (مناد) calls out to them from the heaven: "Arise, for Allah has replaced your evil deeds (سيّئاتكم) with good deeds (حسنات)."

(71) وَمَنْ تابَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً فَإِنَّهُ يَتُوبُ إِلَى اللهِ ** (And whoever repents and acts righteously, he surely turns to Allah as He should be turned to.) He returns to Him مَتاباً. (as He should be turned to.) Al-Qummi says: He does not return to anything of that (sin) with sincerity (إخلاص) and a truthful intention (نيّة صادقة). (72) وَالَّذِينَ لا يَشْهَدُونَ الزُّورَ. (And those who do not bear false witness,) In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): The like of it. And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): The like of it. And Al-Qummi said: It is singing (الغناء) and gatherings of amusement (مجالس اللهو) وَإِذا مَرُّوا بِاللَّغْوِ (1) (and when they come across vain talk,) they pass by nobly (مَرُّوا كِراماً), turning away from it, honoring themselves from dwelling upon it and engaging in it. And among that (is) overlooking indecency (الاغضاء عن الفحشاء), pardoning sins (الصفح عن الذنوب), and using euphemism (الكناية) for that which is deemed repulsive to state explicitly (التصريح به). In al-Majma', narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are those who, when they intend to mention the private parts (الفرج), they use euphemism (كنّوا عنه) for it. And in al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said to some of his companions: "Where have you stayed?" They said: "With so-and-so, the master of al-qiyam (صاحب القيام)." He said: "Be noble (كراماً)." Then He said: "Have you not heard the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in His Book: وَإِذا مَرُّوا بِاللَّغْوِ مَرُّوا كِراماً. (and when they come across vain talk, they pass by nobly.)" And in al-Uyun, narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi 'Abbad, who was known for listening to singing (السماع) and drinking fermented drink (شرب النبيذ). He said: I asked Al-Rida (peace be upon him) about listening to singing (السماع). He said: "The people of Hijaz have an opinion concerning it, but it is within the domain of falsehood (الباطل) and amusement (اللهو). Have you not heard Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: وَإِذا مَرُّوا بِاللَّغْوِ مَرُّوا كِراماً.** (and when they come across vain talk, they pass by nobly.)"

(٧٣) **وَالَّذِينَ إِذا ذُكِّرُوا بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ لَمْ يَخِرُّوا عَلَيْها صُمًّا وَعُمْياناً ** (73) And those who, when reminded of the signs of their Lord, do not fall down before them deaf and blind.

They do not remain upon them deaf and blind, nor are they unseeing (متبصّرين) regarding what is in them, like one who neither hears nor sees. Rather, they incline toward them, listening with attentive ears (آذان واعية), seeing with watchful eyes (عيون راعية).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "They are insightful (مستبصرين), not doubters (شكّاك)."

(٧٤) وَالَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنا هَبْ لَنا مِنْ أَزْواجِنا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنا ** (74) And those who say, 'Our Lord! Give us comfort in our spouses and descendants And it was recited (وقرئ) as وذُرِّيَّتِنا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ ** —and make them a source of comfort to our eyes— by granting them success (توفيق) in obedience (للطّاعة) and the acquisition (حيازة) of virtues (الفضائل). For indeed, when the believer's family shares with him in the obedience of Allah, his heart is gladdened by it, and his eye is comforted (وقرّت عينه) by what he sees of their assistance to him in the religion (الدين) and the expectation of their joining him in Paradise (الجنّة). وَاجْعَلْنا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِماماً. and make us leaders of the Godwary.'

In al-Jawami, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "He meant us (إيّانا)." And in another narration: "It is concerning us (فينا)."

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: "By Allah, this verse is specifically about the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). Most of his supplication was saying: Our Lord! Give us comfort in our spouses (meaning Fatimah) and descendants (al-Hasan and al-Husayn, peace be upon them) —and make them a source of comfort to our eyes—" The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, I did not ask my Lord for a child with a radiant face, nor did I ask for a child with the most beautiful stature. Rather, I asked my Lord for children who are obedient to Allah, fearful and apprehensive of Him, so that when I look at him while he is obedient to Allah, my eye is comforted by him." He said: and make us leaders of the Godwary—we follow the Godwary (المتّقين) who came before us, and the Godwary who come after us follow us."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "We are them, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." He said: And it was narrated: "Indeed, our spouses (أزواجنا) are Khadijah, and our descendants (ذرّياتنا) are Fatimah, and the comfort of our eye (قرّة عيننا) is al-Hasan and al-Husayn, and make us leaders of the Godwary is 'Ali ibn Abi Talib and the Imams (peace be upon them)." He said: And this verse was recited in his presence, so he said: "They have asked Allah for a great thing, that He make them leaders (أئمّة) for the Godwary." He was asked: "How is this, O son of the Messenger of Allah?" He replied: "Allah only revealed: وَاجْعَلْ لَنا مِنَ المُتَّقِينَ اماماً (And make for us a leader from among the Godwary)."

And in al-Jawami, narrated from him (peace be upon him), is that which is close to it.

(٧٥) أُوْلئِكَ يُجْزَوْنَ الْغُرْفَةَ بِما صَبَرُوا ** (75) Those will be rewarded with the chamber for their patience upon the stations of Paradise. وَيُلَقَّوْنَ فِيها ** (and they will be received therein) And it was read (وقرء) with the opening of the yā' and the lightening (التخفيف): تَحِيَّةً وَسَلاماً (with a greeting and a salutation). The angels greet them and offer salutations upon them, or some of them greet others and offer salutations upon him.

(٧٦) خالِدِينَ فِيها ** (abiding in it) They will not die, nor will they exit. حَسُنَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقاماً** (it is a good resting place and abode. 76)

(٧٧) قُلْ ما يَعْبَؤُا بِكُمْ رَبِّي In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: What would My Lord do with you? لَوْ لا دُعاؤُكُمْ In al-Majma', narrated from al-Ayyashi, from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked whether frequent recitation (كثرة القراءة) is better or frequent supplication (كثرة الدعاء). He said: Frequent supplication (كثرة الدعاء) is better, and He recited this Ayah. فَقَدْ كَذَّبْتُمْ ** (You have certainly denied) that which I informed you of, since you opposed Him. فَسَوْفَ يَكُونُ لِزاماً ** (so soon there will be a clinging [punishment].) The recompense for the denial will be unavoidable (لازماً), encompassing you without fail.

قُلْ ما يَعْبَؤُا بِكُمْ رَبِّي لَوْ لا دُعاؤُكُمْ فَقَدْ كَذَّبْتُمْ فَسَوْفَ يَكُونُ لِزاماً ٧٧ (Say, 'My Lord would not care for you were it not for your prayers.' You have certainly denied, so soon there will be a clinging [punishment]. 77)

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Whoever recites this Surah every night, Allah will never punish him nor hold him accountable, and his abode will be in al-Firdaws al-A'la (the Highest Paradise). O Allah, grant us its recitation.
